abstract.para: This is a standalone article fixture for testing article rendering.

article.para: The article starts directly with content, unlike a book which has chapters.

section.title: Introduction
section.para: Articles are simpler than books — they have sections but no chapters. This makes them suitable for shorter documents like blog posts, white papers, and technical notes.

section.title: Code Examples
section.para: Articles can contain the same block elements as chapters:
section.programlisting: def hello(): print("Hello from an article!")

section.title: Conclusion
section.para: Articles work well for focused, self-contained content.
